output.content:
Meeting title: Sprint Planning - Customer Feedback Dashboard
Date: 2026-05-11

Participants:
- Oliver
- Emma
- Noah
- Sofia

Transcript:

Emma: The goal for this sprint is to prepare the first version of the customer feedback dashboard.

Oliver: I can implement the API endpoint for retrieving categorized feedback. I should be able to finish it by Friday.

Emma: Good. Then we will use the existing feedback database for the first prototype instead of creating a new database.

Noah: I can work on the dashboard UI. I will create the first version before next Wednesday.

Sofia: We still need to decide whether the dashboard should support exporting results to CSV in the first release.

Emma: Let us treat CSV export as an open question for now. It may delay the prototype if we include it too early.

Oliver: The sentiment labels are sometimes inconsistent, so the dashboard numbers might not be fully reliable yet.

Emma: That is a risk. We should mention that the first dashboard version is only for internal testing.

Noah: Should we include charts in the first prototype?

Emma: Yes, we should include a simple bar chart showing positive, neutral, and negative feedback counts.

Sofia: I can write a short test plan for the dashboard before the sprint review.

Emma: Great. Sofia owns the test plan, but we did not agree on a specific deadline.

Oliver: I will also add basic error handling to the API if the feedback service does not respond.

Emma: Perfect. The main decisions are that we use the existing database, include a simple sentiment chart, and keep CSV export unresolved for now.

args.summary: The team planned the first version of the customer feedback dashboard, agreeing to use the existing database, include a simple sentiment chart, and assign tasks for API development, UI creation, and test planning. They also identified the need to defer decisions on CSV export and noted data reliability risks.

output:
# Sprint Planning - Customer Feedback Dashboard

## Summary
The team planned the first version of the customer feedback dashboard, agreeing to use the existing database, include a simple sentiment chart, and assign tasks for API development, UI creation, and test planning. They also identified the need to defer decisions on CSV export and noted data reliability risks.

## Decisions
| Decision | Status | Evidence |
|---|---|---|
| Use the existing feedback database for the first prototype. | explicit | "Then we will use the existing feedback database for the first prototype instead of creating a new database." |
| Include a simple bar chart showing positive, neutral, and negative feedback counts. | explicit | "Yes, we should include a simple bar chart showing positive, neutral, and negative feedback counts." |
| The first dashboard version is only for internal testing. | explicit | "We should mention that the first dashboard version is only for internal testing." |
| Keep CSV export unresolved for now. | explicit | "Let us treat CSV export as an open question for now." |

## Action Items
| Task | Owner | Deadline | Status | Context | Evidence |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| Implement the API endpoint for retrieving categorized feedback. | Oliver | Friday | explicit | To support the dashboard prototype. | "I can implement the API endpoint for retrieving categorized feedback. I should be able to finish it by Friday." |
| Create the first version of the dashboard UI. | Noah | before next Wednesday | explicit | For the prototype. | "I can work on the dashboard UI. I will create the first version before next Wednesday." |
| Write a short test plan for the dashboard. | Sofia | Not specified | explicit | To prepare for the sprint review. | "I can write a short test plan for the dashboard before the sprint review." |
| Add basic error handling to the API if the feedback service does not respond. | Oliver | Not specified | explicit | To ensure API reliability. | "I will also add basic error handling to the API if the feedback service does not respond." |

## Open Questions
| Question | Status | Evidence |
|---|---|---|
| Whether the dashboard should support exporting results to CSV in the first release. | explicit | "We still need to decide whether the dashboard should support exporting results to CSV in the first release." |

## Risks
| Risk | Status | Evidence |
|---|---|---|
| The sentiment labels are sometimes inconsistent, which might make the dashboard numbers unreliable. | explicit | "The sentiment labels are sometimes inconsistent, so the dashboard numbers might not be fully reliable yet." |

output.content:
Meeting title: Website Launch Follow-up
Date: 2026-05-12

Participants:
- Maria
- Oliver
- Jonas

Transcript:

Maria: We need to prepare the website for the public launch.

Oliver: I can review the deployment pipeline and make sure the staging environment matches production.

Maria: Good. Oliver owns the deployment review.

Jonas: I will update the landing page copy before Friday.

Maria: Great. We also agreed that the launch will use the current design instead of waiting for the new brand package.

Oliver: There is still a risk that the contact form fails under higher traffic because we have not load tested it.

Maria: Let us keep the load test as a follow-up topic, but we are not assigning it today.

Jonas: Should we include a newsletter signup on the first launch version?

Maria: Not yet. We will postpone newsletter signup until after the launch.

Meeting title: Internal Tool Planning
Date: 2026-05-13

Participants:
- Emma
- Lars
- Priya

Transcript:

Emma: The support team needs a small internal tool to search customer tickets faster.

Lars: We should create a simple search page with filters for customer ID, status, and date range.

Priya: I agree. The first version should only search closed tickets. Searching open tickets can come later.

Emma: Good. So we decided that version one will only search closed tickets.

Lars: Someone needs to write the technical setup guide before Monday.

Priya: I can prepare the database migration script by Thursday.

Emma: The main risk is that the ticket data has inconsistent status values, which could make the filters unreliable.

Lars: We also need to decide whether the tool should support exporting search results.

Emma: Let us leave export support unresolved for now.

Key decisions included limiting the initial version to closed tickets, and action items were assigned for technical setup and database migration. A major risk identified was inconsistent ticket status data.

output.content:
Meeting title: Architecture Discussion - Notification Service
Date: 2026-05-14

Participants:
- Oliver
- Emma
- Viktor

Transcript:

Emma: Today we are only discussing the architecture direction for the notification service. We are not assigning implementation tasks yet.

Oliver: I think the service should expose a REST API for sending notification requests.

Viktor: I agree, but the actual delivery should happen asynchronously through a message queue.

Emma: Then the decision is that the notification service will receive requests through REST and process delivery asynchronously.

Oliver: Should we use RabbitMQ or Azure Service Bus?

Emma: That is still unresolved. We need more information before deciding.

Viktor: A risk is that retries could send duplicate notifications if idempotency is not handled correctly.

Emma: Good point. We should capture that as a risk, but again, no action items today.

Meeting title: Mobile App Release Planning
Date: 2026-05-15

Participants:
- Sara
- Oliver
- Mikkel

Transcript:

Sara: We need to decide whether push notifications should be included in the first mobile release.

Mikkel: I think they are important, but they may delay the release.

Oliver: Could we include only basic push notifications for order updates?

Sara: Yes, let us include basic push notifications for order updates only. Marketing notifications can wait.

Mikkel: I will implement the push notification setup by next Thursday.

Oliver: I will update the API endpoint for notification preferences by Tuesday.

Sara: The risk is that app store approval may take longer than expected.

Mikkel: Should we include in-app chat in the first release?

Sara: No. In-app chat is out of scope for the first release.

Meeting title: Payment Integration Meeting
Date: 2026-05-16

Participants:
- Emma
- Oliver
- Nadia

Transcript:

Emma: We need to prepare the payment integration for the checkout flow.

Oliver: I can add the payment provider abstraction layer by Friday.

Nadia: I will prepare test cards and payment test scenarios before next Wednesday.

Emma: We have decided to use the existing checkout service instead of creating a new checkout service.

Oliver: One unresolved question is whether we should support MobilePay in the first version.

Nadia: We also need to know whether refunds should be handled manually or through the provider API.

Emma: Let us leave both of those open until we have pricing and API information from the provider.

Oliver: The main risk is that provider API limits could affect checkout performance during peak traffic.

args.summary: The meeting focused on preparing the payment integration for the checkout flow. Key actions included adding a payment provider abstraction layer and preparing test scenarios. Decisions were made regarding the use of the existing checkout service, while two key questions (MobilePay support and refund handling) and one risk (API limits) were left unresolved.

# Payment Integration Meeting

## Summary
The meeting focused on preparing the payment integration for the checkout flow. Key actions included adding a payment provider abstraction layer and preparing test scenarios. Decisions were made regarding the use of the existing checkout service, while two key questions (MobilePay support and refund handling) and one risk (API limits) were left unresolved.

## Decisions
| Decision | Status | Evidence |
|---|---|---|
| Use the existing checkout service instead of creating a new one. | explicit | "We have decided to use the existing checkout service instead of creating a new checkout service." |

## Action Items
| Task | Owner | Deadline | Status | Context | Evidence |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| Add the payment provider abstraction layer. | Oliver | Friday | explicit | To prepare the payment integration for the checkout flow. | "I can add the payment provider abstraction layer by Friday." |
| Prepare test cards and payment test scenarios. | Nadia | next Wednesday | explicit | To prepare the payment integration for the checkout flow. | "I will prepare test cards and payment test scenarios before next Wednesday." |

## Open Questions
| Question | Status | Evidence |
|---|---|---|
| Should MobilePay be supported in the first version? | explicit | "One unresolved question is whether we should support MobilePay in the first version." |
| Should refunds be handled manually or through the provider API? | explicit | "We also need to know whether refunds should be handled manually or through the provider API." |

## Risks
| Risk | Status | Evidence |
|---|---|---|
| Provider API limits could affect checkout performance during peak traffic. | explicit | "The main risk is that provider API limits could affect checkout performance during peak traffic." |

Meeting title: Data Import Planning
Date: 2026-05-17

Participants:
- Oliver
- Emma
- Daniel

Transcript:

Emma: We need to import old customer records into the new CRM.

Daniel: The old CSV files have different column names depending on which department exported them.

Oliver: I can write a mapping script that normalizes the column names before import. I can finish that by Friday.

Emma: Good. We decided that the first import will include only active customers.

Daniel: We still need to decide whether archived customers should be imported later.

Oliver: Some records may also be missing email addresses.

Emma: Then the import script should skip records without email addresses for now.

Daniel: I can prepare a sample CSV file for testing by Wednesday.

Meeting title: Admin Dashboard Scope Discussion
Date: 2026-05-18

Participants:
- Emma
- Oliver
- Freja

Transcript:

Emma: We need to decide what should be included in the first admin dashboard release.

Oliver: I think user management should be included from day one.

Freja: I disagree. User management may be too large for the first release.

Emma: Let us say for now that the first version focuses on viewing reports only.

Oliver: Later in the meeting, I still think we should include at least basic user management.

Freja: I do not think we agreed on that.

Emma: Correct. We agreed on reports for the first version, but user management remains unresolved.

Freja: I will create the report overview wireframe by Friday.

Oliver: I can list the required report filters, but I do not have a deadline yet.

Emma: A risk is that unclear scope could delay the dashboard.

args.summary: The meeting focused on defining the scope for the first release of the admin dashboard. It was decided that the initial version will focus on viewing reports, though the inclusion of user management remains unresolved.

Meeting notes - Bug Triage
Date: 2026-05-19

People: Oliver, Emma, Noah

Notes:
- Production bug: users sometimes get logged out after refreshing the page.
- Emma says this is probably related to session cookie settings.
- Decision: patch current authentication service instead of replacing it.
- Oliver -> inspect cookie expiration config. Deadline: tomorrow.
- Noah -> reproduce issue in staging. Deadline: Friday.
- Question: do we need to rotate all active sessions after the fix? Not decided.
- Risk: if the patch changes cookie behavior, some users may be forced to log in again.
- Emma: do not deploy directly to production. Use staging first.
- No owner assigned for writing release notes.
- Release notes should be ready before deployment.

output.content:
Customer Interview - Reporting Feature
Date: 2026-05-20

Participants:
- Interviewer: Emma
- Customer: Thomas from Northwind Logistics
- Observer: Oliver

Transcript:

Emma: What is the biggest problem with the current reporting workflow?

Thomas: It takes too long to create weekly delivery reports. We export data manually, clean it in Excel, and then send it to managers.

Emma: What would help most?

Thomas: A scheduled report would help. If the system could send a weekly PDF automatically, that would save a lot of time.

Oliver: Would CSV export also be useful?

Thomas: Yes, but PDF is more important for managers.

Emma: Any objections or concerns?

Thomas: I am concerned that automatic reports could include outdated data if the delivery system has not synchronized yet.

Emma: Good point. We should follow up on data freshness requirements.

Oliver: I can summarize the interview notes by Friday.

Emma: I will ask Thomas for an example of the current weekly report.

Thomas: I can send an example tomorrow.

Emma: We have not decided yet whether scheduled reports should be part of the first release.

args.summary: The meeting focused on improving the manual and time-consuming process of creating weekly delivery reports. The customer identified scheduled PDF reports as the primary solution, while the team noted concerns regarding data freshness and the scope of the initial release.

decisions.decision: Scheduled reporting is a high priority requirement.

evidence.quote: A scheduled report would help. If the system could send a weekly PDF automatically, that would save a lot of time.

decisions.decision: The team must follow up on data freshness requirements.

evidence.quote: We should follow up on data freshness requirements.

evidence.quote: We have not decided yet whether scheduled reports should be part of the first release.

# Customer Interview - Reporting Feature

## Summary
The meeting focused on improving the manual and time-consuming process of creating weekly delivery reports. The customer identified scheduled PDF reports as the primary solution, while the team noted concerns regarding data freshness and the scope of the initial release.

## Decisions
| Decision | Status | Evidence |
|---|---|---|
| Scheduled reporting is a high priority requirement. | explicit | "A scheduled report would help. If the system could send a weekly PDF automatically, that would save a lot of time." |
| The team must follow up on data freshness requirements. | explicit | "We should follow up on data freshness requirements." |
| The decision regarding whether scheduled reports should be part of the first release is pending. | explicit | "We have not decided yet whether scheduled reports should be part of the first release." |

## Action Items
| Task | Owner | Deadline | Status | Context | Evidence |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| Summarize the interview notes. | Oliver | Friday | explicit | To help the team understand the discussion points. | "I can summarize the interview notes by Friday." |
| Send an example of the current weekly report. | Thomas | tomorrow | explicit | For Emma to review the current manual reporting workflow. | "I can send an example tomorrow." |
| Follow up on data freshness requirements. | Emma | Not specified | explicit | To ensure that automatic reports use synchronized and up-to-date data. | "We should follow up on data freshness requirements." |

## Open Questions
| Question | Status | Evidence |
|---|---|---|
| What are the specific data freshness requirements for automatic reports? | explicit | "I am concerned that automatic reports could include outdated data if the delivery system has not synchronized yet." |

## Risks
| Risk | Status | Evidence |
|---|---|---|
| Automatic reports could include outdated data if the delivery system has not synchronized yet. | explicit | "I am concerned that automatic reports could include outdated data if the delivery system has not synchronized yet." |
